# Authority Matrix


> **Unofficial AI-generated analysis. Not affiliated with the U.S. Air Force or DoD. For research purposes only.**

*Auto-generated by analyze_corpus.py — do not edit manually.*



# PART 1 — AUTHORITY MATRIX TABLE

| Role / Office | Action / Function | Conditions or Limits | Source AFI | Section |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Appropriate Tier Waiver Approval Authority | Approve waiver requests submitted through chain of command | Tiered compliance items only; alternately, AFGM OPR for non-tiered items | AFGM 2026-36-2033 | Waiver guidance |
| The AF (Department of the Air Force) | Approve pre-accession requests for accommodation of religious practices | Unless accommodation adversely affects military necessity (unit readiness, individual readiness, unit cohesion, good order, discipline, health, and/or safety); IAW DAFI 52-201 | AFGM 2026-36-2033 | Religious accommodation |
| Applicant's attending physician + waiver authority | Approve participation in training prior to 6 months postpartum | Member must sign contractual statement IAW DAFMAN 36-2032; written physician approval required with waiver authority concurrence | AFGM 2026-36-2033 | Postpartum training |
| Appropriate Tier Waiver Authority / Requestor's Commander | Approve waiver requests submitted through chain of command | Tiered items to tier waiver authority; non-tiered items to requestor's commander | AFI 36-1301 | Waiver guidance |
| Appropriate senior level official within the command | Approve position and tenure waivers for key leadership positions (non-SAE positions) | Applies to all key leadership positions not identified in para 1.4.4; must provide copy of approved waiver to SAF/AQH | AFI 36-1301 | 1.7.2 / Position waivers |
| Service Acquisition Executive (SAE) | Approve selection of PEO, Deputy PEO, ACAT I PM, ACAT I Deputy PM, and ACAT II PMs | Limited to Acquisition and Business Systems Category I and ACAT II programs | AFI 36-1301 | 1.4.4 |
| Hiring authority | Retain final selection approval authority for key leadership positions not in para 1.4.4 | All other key leadership positions covered in the AFI | AFI 36-1301 | 1.4.5 |
| Service Acquisition Executive (SAE) | Approve all position waivers for PEO, Deputy PEO, ACAT I PM/Deputy PM, ACAT II PMs | Limited to specified acquisition program positions | AFI 36-1301 | Position waivers |
| Service Acquisition Executive (SAE) | Approve waiver to release an individual from tenure agreement before it ends | If warranted; case-by-case basis | AFI 36-1301 | 1.7.2.1 |
| Service Acquisition Executive (SAE) | Approve all tenure waivers for PEO, Deputy PEO, ACAT I PM/Deputy PM, ACAT II PMs | Limited to specified acquisition program positions | AFI 36-1301 | Tenure waivers |
| CMSAF | Receive/approve non-tiered waiver requests for AFI 36-2109 | Non-tiered compliance items routed through chain of command | AFI 36-2109 | Waiver guidance |
| Appropriate Tier Waiver Approval Authority | Approve tiered waiver requests for AFI 36-2109 | Tiered compliance items; through chain of command | AFI 36-2109 | Waiver guidance |
| 12 OAY (Outstanding Airmen of the Year) | Bring spouse, family member, or one person of their choosing | Each 12 OAY is individually authorized | AFI 36-2109 | OAY entitlements |
| Appropriate Tier Waiver Authority / Requestor's Commander | Approve waiver requests | Tiered items to tier waiver authority; non-tiered items to requestor's commander | AFI 36-2113 | Waiver guidance |
| Approval authority (unnamed role — context: 8F000 SDI authority) | Approve RegAF requests to serve beyond four years in the 8F000 SDI | RegAF members only | AFI 36-2113 | 2.1.13 / 2.1.14 |
| Approval authority (unnamed role — context: 8F000 SDI authority) | Approve RegAF applications for members currently serving in another SDI | RegAF members only | AFI 36-2113 | 2.1.14 / 2.1.15 |
| CCM + Approval Authority + WG/CC or equivalent | Approve over-grade requests during rotation | Subject to FY manning policy in effect at the time; recommendation by CCM required, approval by WG/CC or equivalent | AFI 36-2113 | 6.4.7 |
| AFPC/DP2OSS | Waive codes to facilitate needs of the Air Force | At AFPC/DP2OSS discretion | AFI 36-2113 | 6.8.3 |
| Appropriate Tier Waiver Authority / AFPC/DPM | Approve waiver requests for AFI 36-2122 | Tiered items to tier waiver authority; non-tiered items staffed through applicable MAJCOM to AFPC/DPM for final decision | AFI 36-2122 | Waiver guidance |
| Appropriate Tier Waiver Authority / Publication OPR | Approve waiver requests for AFI 36-2129 | Tiered items to tier waiver authority; non-tiered items to publication OPR | AFI 36-2129 | Waiver guidance |
| Appropriate Tier Waiver Authority / AFPC/DP3AM | Approve waiver requests for AFI 36-2135 | Tiered items to tier waiver authority; non-tiered items through MAJCOM to AFPC/DP3AM | AFI 36-2135 | Waiver guidance |
| AFPC/DPPSP (with concurrence of AF/A1P, AF/REP, or NGB/A1P as applicable) | Approve non-tiered waiver requests for AFI 36-2406 | Tiered items to appropriate tier waiver authority; non-tiered items require concurrence of AF/A1P (or AF/REP / NGB/A1P for ARC/ANG) | AFI 36-2406 | Waiver guidance |
| Wing Commander or equivalent (delegable no lower than Deputy WG/CC) | Make final approval/disapproval decision on requests when the initial approval authority recommends disapproval | Only when initial approval authority recommends disapproval and provides justification; delegation floor is Deputy WG/CC or equivalent | AFI 36-2406 | Escalation provision |
| AFPC/DPPSP | Serve as approval authority for RegAF members requesting consideration for non-rated periods of supervision (courses) | RegAF members only | AFI 36-2406 | 1.4.11 area |
| ARPC/DPTSE | Serve as approval authority for ARC members requesting consideration for non-rated periods of supervision (courses) | ARC members only | AFI 36-2406 | 1.4.11 area |
| Approval authority (for criminal conviction comment waivers) | Approve waiver of mandatory comments for specific criminal convictions on evaluations | Must issue written finding that mandatory comments are not in best interests of the AF and would unduly harm the ratee; decision is final and not appealable | AFI 36-2406 | 1.8.1.1.1.3.1 |
| Referral reviewer | Grant extension of time for ratee rebuttal | When ratee needs additional time (e.g., ADC unavailability or rebuttal returned for exceeding 10 pages) | AFI 36-2406 | Referral process |
| Ratee | Grant access to completed evaluation forms | At ratee's discretion | AFI 36-2406 | 2.9.3 |
| Rater or HLR | Use stratification statement from someone between the rater and HLR | Only when the stratification comes from someone in the chain between rater and HLR; either the rater or the HLR (not both) is authorized | AFI 36-2406 | 3.15.6.7.2 |
| AFELM/CC (on G-series orders) | Sign ALQ evaluations in lieu of forced distributor | Joint agencies only; exception applies when forced distributor signs the MEL | AFI 36-2406 | 4.12.5.4 |
| AFPC MLR office | Approve exceptions to maintain integrity in the Officer Evaluation System | To ensure fair and proper consideration for all affected officers | AFI 36-2406 | MLR provisions |
| AFPC MLR office | Approve changes to evaluation records up to one duty day prior to central selection board | Extreme circumstances only; case-by-case basis | AFI 36-2406 | Pre-board changes |
| ARPC/PB | Approve changes to evaluation records up to one duty day prior to central selection board | Extreme circumstances only; case-by-case basis (ARC members) | AFI 36-2406 | Pre-board changes |
| ERAB (Evaluation Reports Appeals Board) | Modify evaluations that differ from the applicant's request | May direct a different remedy than what the applicant requested (e.g., correct feedback date instead of voiding report) | AFI 36-2406 | 10.2.4 |
| AFPC or ARPC | Make corrections to digitally signed evaluations after transmission | Only AFPC or ARPC authorized once evaluation has been digitally signed and transmitted | AFI 36-2406 | Minor Errors rule |
| CSAF | Approve exceptions to AFI 36-2406 requirements | General authority for exceptions | AFI 36-2406 | Exceptions |
| HAF Deputy Chief of Staff, Personnel | Exercise exception authority similar to CSAF | Limited to cases involving the management levels of general officers | AFI 36-2406 | Exceptions |

# PART 2 — CONFLICTING OR AMBIGUOUS AUTHORITIES

- **Non-tiered waiver routing is inconsistent across publications.** AFGM 2026-36-2033 routes non-tiered waivers to the "AFGM OPR"; AFI 36-1301 and AFI 36-2113 route them to "the requestor's commander"; AFI 36-2109 routes them to the CMSAF; AFI 36-2122 routes them to AFPC/DPM via MAJCOM; AFI 36-2135 routes them to AFPC/DP3AM via MAJCOM; AFI 36-2406 routes them to AFPC/DPPSP with AF/A1P concurrence; and AFI 36-2129 routes them to the "publication OPR." While each applies to its own publication, the lack of a single standard creates confusion about the general principle for non-tiered waiver approval. — See AFGM 2026-36-2033 vs AFI 36-1301 vs AFI 36-2109 vs AFI 36-2113 vs AFI 36-2122 vs AFI 36-2129 vs AFI 36-2135 vs AFI 36-2406.

- **"Approval authority" for the 8F000 SDI (First Sergeant) is ambiguously identified.** AFI 36-2113, paragraphs 2.1.13–2.1.15, reference an approval authority for RegAF requests to serve beyond four years in the 8F000 SDI and for applications from members in another SDI, but the specific role/office serving as that approval authority is not named in the extracted statements. Without the full context of section 2.1, it is unclear whether this is AFPC, AF/A1, the MAJCOM/CC, or another office. — See AFI 36-2113 §§ 2.1.14–2.1.15.

- **Over-grade approval during rotation has ambiguous authority chain.** AFI 36-2113, para 6.4.7 states it requires "a recommendation by the CCM, approval authority, WG/CC or equivalent." It is unclear whether the CCM, the "approval authority," and the WG/CC are three separate roles in a sequential chain, or whether "approval authority" is synonymous with "WG/CC or equivalent." The comma-delimited construction is ambiguous. — See AFI 36-2113 § 6.4.7.

- **Position and tenure waiver authority for "other key leadership positions" uses vague role designation.** AFI 36-1301 assigns this to "the appropriate senior level official within the command" without specifying the minimum grade, position, or title required to qualify as that official. This could be interpreted differently across commands. — See AFI 36-1301 (position/tenure waiver paragraphs).

- **Escalation of disapproved requests to Wing Commander may overlap with initial approval authority.** AFI 36-2406 states that if the "approval authority" recommends disapproval, the request is forwarded to the "wing commander or equivalent." In cases where the wing commander IS the initial approval authority, the escalation path is unclear. — See AFI 36-2406 (disapproval escalation provision).

- **CSAF vs. HAF Deputy Chief of Staff, Personnel — parallel exception authority with unclear boundary.** AFI 36-2406 states only the CSAF may approve exceptions, but then grants similar authority to the HAF DCS/Personnel "in cases involving the management levels of general officers." It is unclear whether DCS/Personnel authority is exclusive to general officer cases (i.e., CSAF retains no role in GO cases) or concurrent (both can act). — See AFI 36-2406 (Exceptions provision).

- **AFI 36-2406 references a cross-reference ("See paragraph 1.4.11.1.2 for the approval authority") without specifying who the approval authority is in the extracted text.** The actual authority assignment depends on the un-extracted paragraph. — See AFI 36-2406 § 1.4.11.

---

# PART 3 — AUTHORITY GAPS

- **Selection authority for CMSAF, CCM, and Group SEL positions is referenced but not specified.** AFI 36-2109 states it "establishes the selection and approval authority" for these positions but the extracted statements do not identify which specific role or office holds that selection/approval authority.

- **No authority is identified for approving religious accommodation requests at the pre-accession stage.** AFGM 2026-36-2033 states "the AF will approve" such requests, but does not specify which office, role, or organizational level within the AF makes the approval decision.

- **Waiver authority for postpartum training participation is referenced but not identified.** AFGM 2026-36-2033 requires "concurrence from the waiver authority" for members wishing to train prior to 6 months postpartum, but does not specify which role or office constitutes that waiver authority.

- **The documentation and/or approval authority "will vary depending on the nature of the circumstances" (AFI 36-2406) but no matrix or decision tree is provided in the extracted text to identify which authority applies under which circumstances.** This creates a gap where personnel cannot determine the correct approval authority without additional guidance.

- **No clear authority is assigned for establishing or updating FY manning policy** that governs over-grade approvals during First Sergeant rotations (referenced in AFI 36-2113 § 6.4.7).

- **No authority is identified for the initial approval/disapproval decision on evaluation-related requests** that may be escalated to the Wing Commander under AFI 36-2406's disapproval escalation provision. The "approval authority" at the lower level is referenced generically without role specification.
